(S.) -b3- Hunters that shoot, or cast: (S, K:) sing. ; (K;) accord. to Lth.; [and in the M it is said that signifies a shooter, or caster: or, accord. to some, a hunter: ] but this is a mistake; for قسورة is a coll. n., having no sing.; and Fr says, that in the verse of the Kur cited above, it means shooters, or casters of missile
قسر.summary_en: The root قسر (qasr) primarily means to overpower or subdue. A قَسْوَرَةٌ (qaswarah) is a mighty, strong, and courageous individual, often referring to a lion due to its strength.

قسس.summary_en: The root قسس (qss) primarily refers to hardness or rigidity, like a hard heart. It also relates to being old or aged, and to a Christian priest, perhaps due to their venerable age or strictness.

قشعر.definition_en: Q. 4 إِقْشَعَرَّ ذ , said of the skin, (S, K,) It quaked; shuddered; was, or became, affected by a tremor, quaking, or quivering. (K.) [And in like manner said of a man, (see the part. n., below,) i. e. He quaked, or shuddered. ] -b2- It (the skin) dried up ( قَفَّ ) by reason of mange, or scab. (TA.) -b3- إِقْشَعَرَّتِ الأَرْضُ The earth became of a colour inclining to that of dust, or ashes, ( إِرْبَدَّت ,) and contracted, by reason of drought. (TA.) -b4- اقشعرّت السَّنَةُ (tropical:) The year became one of drought. (K, * TA:)
قشعر.summary_en: The root قشعر describes a trembling or shuddering, often of the skin, due to fear or cold.

قصف.definition_en: نَبَاتٌ قَصِيفٌ رَيّانُ ذ [ A weak, or fragile, sappy plant ]. (TA, in art. خرع .)
قصف.summary_en: The root Q-S-F describes something weak, fragile, or easily broken. It can refer to a plant that is sappy but delicate, suggesting a lack of strength despite its moisture.

قصم.definition_en: قَيْصُومٌ ذ Artemisia abrotanum, or southern-wood; also called قَيْصُومٌ ذَكَرٌ : and قَيْصُومٌ أُنْثَى is applied to santolina.
قصم.summary_en: The root QSM refers to a plant, specifically Artemisia abrotanum, also known as southernwood. There are male (ذَكَرٌ) and female (أُنْثَى) varieties, with the latter referring to santolina.

قضب.definition_en: قَضْبَةٌ ذ Such as are cut, and eaten in their fresh state, of plants, (M, Msb, K,) of any kind; as is said in the Bári'; (Msb;) a pl. [or rather coll. gen. n.] of which the sing. [or n. un.] is : (K:) or it signifies, (S, O, Msb,) or signifies also, (K,) [ a species of trefoil, or clover; i. e.] i. q. رَطْبَةٌ , (S, O, Msb,) which is the same as فِصْفِصَة , (Msb,) or قَتٌّ , [which is also the same,] (K,) the name by which the people of Mekkeh call قَتّ , (Fr, TA,) and (K) called in Pers. (S, O) إِسْفِسْت , (S, Mgh, K,) or إِسْبِسْت ; (O;) as also ; (S;) or this is the sing. [or rather n. un.] of قَضْبٌ , which signifies فَصَافِصُ [pl. of فِصْفِصَةٌ ]; (M;) called قَضْبٌ because it is cut. (Mgh.) -b2- And Any tree of which the branches grow long and lank: (K, * TA:) بَسَطَتْ in the K is a
قضب.summary_en: The root "qDb" refers to plants that are cut and eaten fresh, like clover or trefoil. It also describes trees with long, lank branches, or specific trees used for making bows and arrows.

قضض.summary_en: The root Q-D-D primarily refers to the sound of something breaking or cracking, like a bone or a dry branch.

قطط.summary_en: The root ق ط ط (q-t-t) primarily refers to cutting or severing something, often into pieces. It can also describe something being short or curtailed, or a cat's purr.

قطمر.definition_en: قِطْمِيرٌ ذ (S, K) and قِطْمَارٌ (K) The cleft ( شَقّ ) of a date-stone: (M, K:) or the integument ( قِشْرَة ) that is upon it ( فِيهَا ): (K:) the thin skin (S, K) called فُوفَة , which is upon a date-stone, (S,) between the stone and the date itself: (K:) or the white point [i. e. the embryo ] in the back of the date-stone, (S, K,) from which [ when it is sown ] the palm-tree grows forth. (S.) -b2- [Hence,] (assumed tropical:) A small, mean, paltry, contemptible, thing. So the former signifies in the Kur, xxxv. 14. One says also مَا أَصَبْتُ مِنْهُ قِطْمِيرًا , meaning, (assumed tropical:) I obtained not of him, or it, anything. (TA.)
قطمر.summary_en: The root قطمر (qTmr) refers to the thin skin or cleft of a date-stone, or the small white point from which a palm tree grows. It metaphorically signifies something small, mean, or paltry.

قعر.definition_en: قَعْرٌ ذ The bottom, lowest depth, or extremity of the lower part, [ of the interior, ] of a thing; (Msb;) i. q. عُمْقٌ , of a well &c.; (S;) the remotest part of anything; (A, K;) [as, for instance,] of a river, (TA,) and of a vessel; (S, A;) pl. قُعُورٌ . (Msb, K.) You say جَلَسَ فِى قَعْرِ بَيْتِهِ , [lit., He sat in the innermost part of his house, ] meaning (assumed tropical:) he kept in his house. (Msb.) And لَا أَدْخُلُ عَلَيْهِ قَعْرَ البَيْتِ , and , and , all signify the same [ I will not go in to him in the innermost part of the house; or (assumed tropical:) I will not be an intimate in his house ]. (TA.) -b2- [ Depth, properly and tropically. You say] ذَهَبَ فى قَعْرِ الأَرْضِ [ It (a tree or the like) went into the depth of the earth ]. (TA.) And فُلَانٌ بَعِيدُ القَعْرِ (A, T
قعر.summary_en: The root قعر (qEr) primarily refers to the bottom or lowest depth of something, like a well or a house. It can also metaphorically describe deep judgment or profound speech.

قفل.definition_en: قَافِلَةٌ ذ A company, or an assemblage of persons, travelling together: (El-Fárábee, Msb:) or a company returning from a journey: (S, K:) and commencing a journey; as auguring their return: (El-Fárábee, Msb, K:) he who restricts it to those returning from a journey errs: (ElFárábee, Msb, in which see more:) a caravan.
قفل.summary_en: A *qāfilah* is a company of people traveling together, often a caravan. It can refer to those starting a journey, as a good omen for their return, or those returning from one.

قلع.definition_en: 1 قَلَعَ ذ and He pulled, plucked, tore, wrenched, or rooted, out, or up, or off; detached; removed from his or its place; displaced; (Msb, K *;) eradicated; uprooted; unrooted. (K.) -b2- تَقْلَعُ (K in art. جذو ) and تقلعُ السَّيْرَ (TA in that art.) [app. for تَقْلَعُ فِى السَّيْرِ ], said of she-camels, (K ib.) [app. They raise their feet clear from the ground: see قَلِعٌ and قُلْعٌ : the pret. seems to be قَلِعَ : so if تَقْلَعُ be the right reading: but in a copy of the K it seems to be تُقْلِعُ : see جَاذٍ , art. جذو .
قلع.summary_en: The root قَلَعَ (qalaʿa) primarily means to pull, pluck, tear, or wrench something out or up, detaching it from its place. It signifies removal, displacement, or uprooting.

قمح.definition_en: 1 قَمِحَهُ ذ , (S, A, L, K,) aor. قَمَحَ , (K,) inf. n. قَمْحٌ ; (S;) and ; (S, A, L, K;) He ate it, or took it into his mouth, (S, K,) namely, meal of parched barley or wheat, &c., (S, A, L,) not bread nor dates nor the like, but only what is eaten in the manner termed سَفٌّ , (L,) without moistening it, or kneading it with water &c.; syn. اِسْتَفَّهُ ; (S, K;) he took it in the palm of his hand (A, L) [ and conveyed it ] to his mouth (A) or licked it up. (L.) And قُمْحَةً مِنْهُ [ I so ate a mouthful thereof, i. e. of what is eaten in the manner described above]. (A.) -b2- And likewise signifies, (L, K,) as also قَمِحَهُ , (L,) He drank it, namely, what is called نَبِيذ , (L, K,) and شَرَاب [app. as meaning wine], and water, and milk. (L.) -A2- And, from قَمِحَهُ signifying as expl. abov
قمح.summary_en: The root قمح (qmH) primarily describes eating dry food without moistening it, or drinking various liquids.

قمطر.definition_en: يَوْمٌ قَمْطَرِيرٌ ذ , and , (S, K,) and , (TA,) A distressful, or calamitous, day: (S, K:) or a day that makes one knit the brow, or contract the skin between the eyes: so the first is explained by some as occurring in the Kur lxxvi. 10. (TA.) -b2- شَرٌّ قَمْطَرِيرٌ , (TA,) and , and , (Lth, TA,) and , (TA,) Intense evil. (Lth, TA.)
قمطر.summary_en: The root قمطر (qmTr) describes something intensely difficult or distressing. A "day qamṭarīr" is a calamitous day that makes one frown. It can also refer to intense evil.

قمع.definition_en: قَمِعُ البُسْرِ ذ What sticks to the date, around its stalk: (Mgh:) the base of the date. (Mgh, art. ذنب .) See ثُفْرُوقٌ . -b2- See also a use of the pl. أَقْمَاع , voce دَرْدَار . -b3- قِمَعُ الأُذُونِ The meatus of the ear: see جُلْجُلاَنٌ .
قمع.summary_en: The root قمع (qmE) refers to the base or stalk of a date, the part that sticks to it. It can also describe the meatus of the ear. The term highlights small, foundational, or attached parts.

قمل.definition_en: قُمَّلٌ ذ i. q. سُوسٌ : or a kind of قُرَاد . (Jel, vii. 130.) See سَاسَ .
قمل.summary_en: The root QML refers to small, destructive insects. It can mean a kind of weevil or woodworm (سُوسٌ), or a type of tick (قُرَاد). The term قُمَّلٌ specifically denotes these tiny pests.

قنو.definition_en: 1 قَنِىَ الحَيَآءَ ذ He kept to the sense of shame, or modesty; (S, K;) he preserved it: and i. q. اِسْتَحْيَى : and he kept to [or preserved ] his sense of shame, or modesty. (TA.) -b2- قَنَا غَنَمًا , and , He took for himself, got, or acquired, sheep, or goats [ for a permanent possession ], not for sale. (JK.) -b3- قَنَا , aor. يَقْنُو , inf. n. قُنُوٌّ ; a dial. var. of قَنَأَ , q. v. (TA.)
قنو.summary_en: The root قنو (qnw) primarily means to keep to or preserve something, especially one's sense of shame or modesty.

قني.summary_en: The root قني (qny) primarily means to acquire or possess something, especially wealth or property, and to keep it for oneself.

قوب.definition_en: قُوبٌ ذ A young bird; (S, A, O, K;) as also and : (K:) or signifies, (S, A, O,) or signifies also, (K,) an egg; (S, A, O, K;) and so does : (K;) is used in the latter sense as meaning ذَاتُ قُوبٍ , i. e. ذَاتُ فَرْخٍ : (Az, * O, TA: *) or it is like رَاضِيَةٌ in the phrase عِيشَةٌ رَاضِيَةٌ [meaning مَرْضِيَّةٌ ]: (A:) [or as being originally the part. n. of قَابَت in the phrase قَابَتِ البَيْضَةُ : and it may be used in the former sense as being originally the act. part. n. of قَابَت in the phrase قَابَتِ البَيْضَةَ said of a hen-bird:] and signifies an egg from which the young bird has come forth: (Az, TA:) or قُوبٌ signifies an egg: and , a young bird: (AHeyth, TA:) the pl. of قُوبٌ is أَقْوَابٌ . (K.) It is said in a prov., بَرِئَتْ مِنْ قُوبٍ , (S, A, O,) or تَخَلَّصَتْ مِنْ قُوبٍ , (
قوب.summary_en: The root قوب (qwb) primarily refers to a young bird or an egg, especially one from which a chick has hatched.

قوس.definition_en: قَوْسٌ ذ [ A bow; ] a certain thing, well known, (A, K,) with which one shoots: (M, TA:) of the fem. gender: (IAmb, M, Msb:) or masc. and fem.: (S, Msb:) or sometimes masc.: (A, K:) pl. [of pauc.] أَقْوَاسٌ (IAmb, S, A, Msb, K) and أَقْيَاسٌ , (TA, and so in some copies of the K, in the place of the former,) the ى being interchangeable with the و , (TA,) and [of mult.] قِسِىٌّ , (S, A, Msb, K,) originally قُوُوسٌ , (S, TA,) which is not used, (TA,) of the neasure فُعُولٌ , (S, Msb,) first changed to قُسُوٌّ , of he measure فُلُوعٌ , and then to قِسِىٌّ , of the measure فِلِيعٌ , like عِصِىٌّ , (S,) and قُسِىٌّ , (Fr, Sgh, K,) from the same original, (TA,) [like عُصِىٌّ ,] and قِيَاسٌ , (IAmb, S, A, Msb, K,) which is more agreeable with analogy than قسىّ . (TA.) The dim. is قُوَيْسٌ , (IAmb
قوس.summary_en: The root "qaws" primarily refers to a bow, a well-known weapon for shooting arrows. It can be masculine or feminine.

كبد.definition_en: كَبِدٌ ذ , (S, L, Msb, K, &c.,) the most chaste and best known form of the word, (TA,) and , (S, L, Msb, K,) a contraction of the former, (Msb,) and , (S, L, K,) also a contraction of the first, (S,) [The liver; ] a certain black piece of flesh on the right of the lungs: (L:) fem., and sometimes masc.; (Fr, L, Msb, K;) or fem. only: (Lh, ISd, L, Msb:) pl. أَكْبَادٌ (S, L, Msb, K) and كُبُودٌ ; (L, Msb, K;) the latter seldom used. (Msb) -b2- Also, [the first,] (tropical:) The place of the liver, outside: (L;) the side. (K) It is said in a trad., فَوَضَعَ يَدَهُ عَلَى كَبِدِى , meaning, And he put his hand upon my side externally; or, upon the external part of my side, next the liver. (L.) -b3- (assumed tropical:) The inside of an animal, altogether. (Kr, ISd, K.) Sometimes used in this sens
كبد.summary_en: The root كبد primarily refers to the liver, a vital organ. Tropically, it extends to mean the "middle" or "main part" of something, like the earth's interior, the sky, or a bow's handle.

كبكب.summary_en: The root كبكب (kbkb) primarily means to throw down or cast down something, especially with force or repeatedly.

كثب.definition_en: 1 كَثَبَهُ ذ , aor. كَثُبَ and كَثِبَ , inf. n. كَثْبٌ , He collected it together, (S, K,) from a near place. (TA.) كُثِبَ بَيْنَنَا [ It (referring to a quantity of dates, sent by Mohammad,) was collected together among us; ] meaning, it was left collected together before us. (TA, from a trad.) كَثَبَ القَوْمَ He collected together the people. (Msb.) -b2- كَثَبَهُ , aor. كَثُبَ and كَثِبَ , inf. n. كَثْبٌ , He poured it out, or forth. (K.) He collected it together from a near place, and poured it out, or forth. (TA.) -b3- He scattered dust, or earth, part over part. (Lth.) -b4- كَثَبَ , aor. كَثُبَ , inf. n. كَثْبٌ , He scattered corn or other food. (AZ.) -b5- كَثَبَ كِنَانَتَهُ He turned his quiver upside down, and scattered its contents. (K.) In one copy of the K, explained by نكثها ; b
كثب.summary_en: The root كثب primarily means to collect things together, often from a nearby place, or to pour something out. It can also describe scattering, like dust or grain, or a group of people gathering.

كدر.definition_en: 1 كَدِرَ ذ , aor. كَدَرَ ; and كَدُرَ , aor. كَدُرَ ; (S, A, Msb, K, &c.;) and كَدَرَ ; (Sgh, K;) but this last is said in the L to be allowable only as signifying “ he poured out ” water; (TA;) inf. n. كَدَرٌ , (S, A, Msb, K,) of the first, (S, Msb,) or second, (TA,) and كُدُورَةٌ , (S, A, Msb, K,) of the second, (S, Msb,) and كَدَارَةٌ , (K,) also of the second, (TA,) and كُدُورٌ , and كُدْرَةٌ , (K,) or the last is a simple subst.; (TA;) and ; (S, Msb, K;) and , inf. n. إِكْدِرَارٌ ; (K;) and ; (Bd lxxxi. 2;) It (water, S, Msb, &c.) was, or became, turbid, thick, or muddy; contr. of صَفَا ; (S, A, K;) it ceased to be clear: (Msb:) or كُدْرَةٌ relates to colour, (K,) specially; (TA;) and كُدُورَةٌ , to water, (K,) and to life, العَيْش ; in the K, العَيْن , but this is a mistake; (TA;) an
كدر.summary_en: The root كدر primarily describes water becoming turbid or muddy, losing its clarity. It also extends to life becoming troublesome or perturbed, and a complexion becoming dusky.

كدي.summary_en: The root كدي primarily means to be difficult, hard, or to toil. It implies struggling to obtain something, like digging in hard ground or being stingy.

كسد.definition_en: 1 كَسَدَ ذ , (S, L, Msb, K,) aor. كَسُدَ , (L, Msb,) inf. n. كَسَادٌ (S, L, Msb, K) and كُسُودٌ ; (K;) and كَسُدَ ; (L, K;) but the former is the verb in common use; (TA;) It (a thing, S, Msb, a commodity, &c., L) was, or became, unsaleable, or difficult of sale, and in little demand. (L, Msb, K.) The original meaning is It was, or became, in a bad, corrupt, or unsound state. (T, Msb.) -A2- كَسَدَتِ السُّوقُ , (aor. كَسُدَ , inf. n. كَسَادٌ , L,) The market was, or became, stagnant, or dull, with respect to traffic. (S, * A, L, Msb, K.) See 4.
كسد.summary_en: The root كَسَدَ (kasada) primarily means something became unsaleable, difficult to sell, or in little demand. It also describes a market becoming stagnant or dull.

كشط.definition_en: 1 كَشَطَ ذ , (S, Msb, K,) aor. كَشِطَ , (Msb, MS,) inf. n. كَشْطٌ , (Msb, K,) He removed, put off, took off, or stripped off, (S, Msb, K,) a thing (Msb, K) from ( عَنْ ) a thing which it covered; (K;) as, for instance, the housing, or covering, (S, K,) from a horse, (K,) or from the back of a horse; (S;) and the cover from a thing; (S;) and the skin from a slaughtered camel: (TA:) and قَشَطَ is a dial. var. thereof; (Yaakoob, S;) the former being of the dial. of Kureysh, (Yaakoob, accord. to the TA,) or of Keys, (M in art. قشط ,) and the latter of the dial. of Temeem and Asad; the ك not being a substitute for the ق : (Yaakoob, TA:) and signifies the same. (Ham., p. 693.) It is said in the Kur, [lxxxi. 11,] وَإِذَا السَّمَآءُ كُشِطَتْ And when the heaven shall be removed from its place, li
كشط.summary_en: The root كشط (kshT) means to remove, strip off, or pull off a covering from something, like a saddle from a horse or skin from a camel. It's used in the Quran to describe the sky being removed.

كفأ.summary_en: The term "مَكْفُوْءُ اللّون" refers to something whose color has been turned over or changed. It is related to "كَفِىْءٌ," which implies a state of being turned over or inverted.

كفت.definition_en: 1 كَفَتَ ذ , [aor. كَفِتَ ,] inf. n. كَفْتٌ , It (a thing) turned over, lit, back for belly: (K:) or, as in a copy of the L, he turned a thing over, back for belly. (TA.) -b2- كَفَتَهُ , (K,) or كَفَتَهُ عَنْ وَجْهِهِ , (S,) aor. كَفِتَ , (K,) inf. n. كَفْتٌ ; and ; (TA;) He turned him away, averted him, or diverted him, from his course, or design. (S, K.) (You say) كَفَتَهُ عَنْ حَاجَتِهِ He withheld him, restrained him, or debarred him, from the thing that he wanted. (As.) -b3- اللّٰهُ God took him; syn. قَبَضَهُ : meaning he died: and so كفتة اللّٰه اليه . (TA.) -A2- كَفَتٌ , [aor. كَفِتَ , (S, K) inf. n. كَفْتٌ and كِفَاتٌ and كَفِيتٌ and كَفَتَانٌ , (K,) He, or it, hastened, or was quick, or swift: (S:) it (a bird &c.) hastened, or was quick, or swift, in flying, and running, and cont
كفت.summary_en: The root كفت (kft) primarily means to turn something over or away, to hasten, or to draw something together.

كلأ.summary_en: The root كُلْأَةٌ (kulʾah) refers to pasturage or herbage, something that is guarded or preserved. It is related to the idea of protection and sustenance for livestock.

كلح.definition_en: 1 كَلَحَ ذ , aor. كَلَحَ , inf. n. كُلُوحٌ and كُلَاحٌ ; (S, K;) and , and , (K,) and ; (A;) He (a man, S) grinned, or displayed his teeth, (M, rendered in the S and K by تَكَشَّرَ ), frowning, or contracting his face, or looking sternly, austerely, or morosely. (S, M, K.) -b2- كَلَحَ فِى وَجْهِهِ He frightened him; namely a child, and a madman. (A.)
كلح.summary_en: The root كَلَحَ (kalaha) describes a person who grins, showing their teeth, often with a frowning or stern facial expression.

كند.definition_en: كَنُودٌ ذ Ungrateful; who disacknowledges benefits; (El-Kelbee, S, A, L, K;) as also : (L, K;) or a denier: (L:) the former applied also to a woman; and so : (S, A, L:) an unbeliever: (Zj, L:) a blamer of his Lord, (El-Hasan, L, K,) who takes account of evil accidents and forgets benefits: (El-Hasan, L:) rebellious. or disobedient, (K,) in the dial. of Kindeh: (TA:) niggardly; tenacious; avaricious; (K;) in the dial. of the Benoo-Málik: (TA:) who eats alone, and withholds his drinking-bowl ( رَِفْدَهُ ), and beats his slave: (Kh, L, K:) all these meanings are assigned to it in the verse [6 of ch. c.] of the Kur-án, إِنَّ ٱلْإِنْسَانَ لِرَبِّهِ لَكَنُودٌ : but of the last, ISd remarks, that he knows no foundation for it in the classical language, and that it is not easily admissible coupled
كند.summary_en: The root كَنُودٌ describes someone ungrateful, who denies blessings and blames God. It can also mean rebellious, niggardly, or even land that produces nothing.

كنس.definition_en: 1 كَنَسَ ذ , (S, A, Mgh, Msb,) aor. كَنُسَ , (S, Msb,) or كَنِسَ , (Mgh,) inf. n. كَنْسٌ , (S, Mgh, Msb,) He swept (Mgh, TA) a house, or chamber, (S, A, Mgh, Msb,) or place, (TA,) with a مِكْنَسَة [or broom]. (A, Mgh.) -b2- مَرُّوا بِهِمْ فَكَنَسُوهُمْ (tropical:) They passed by them and swept them away, or destroyed them; syn. كَسَحُوهُمْ . (A, TA.) -A2- كَنَسَ , (S, A, Mgh, Msb, K,) aor. كَنِسَ , (S, Msb, K,) or كَنُسَ , (Mgh,) inf. n. كُنُوسٌ , (Mgh, Msb,) He (an antelope) entered his كِنَاس , (S, A, Mgh, Msb, K,) i. e., his covert, or hiding-place, among trees; (S, K;) or abode; (Msb;) or cave; (TA;) as also (S, A, Mgh, K) and ;) (A, TA;) which two verbs are likewise said of a wild bull or cow, in the same sense. (TA.) [Hence,] also signifies (tropical:) He (a man, TA) entered the tent
كنس.summary_en: The root كنس (kns) primarily means "to sweep" a place clean. It also describes an antelope entering its hiding place or a person entering a tent.

كوي.summary_en: The root كوي primarily means to iron clothes, to press them with heat to smooth them. It also refers to cauterizing, burning with a hot iron for medicinal purposes or punishment.

لحف.definition_en: مِلْحَفَةٌ ذ A مُلَآءَة that is سُمُط [ not lined, nor stuffed ]: if lined or stuffed, the vulgar also call it by this name, but the Arabs do not know this: (L, TA:) and the same applies to the : Az says, that لِحَافٌ and مِلْحَفٌ mean the same: like إِزَارٌ and مِئْزَرٌ , and قِرَامٌ and مِقْرَمٌ ; and sometimes one says مِقْرَمَةٌ and مِلْحَفَةٌ ; and it is the same whether the garment be سُمُط or lined. (TA.) He says also, [in another place,] that the Arabs apply the terms and مِلْحَفَةٌ to A night-wrapper ( إِزَارُ لَيْلِ ) if it be طَاقٌ وَاحِدٌ [ a single piece of stuff; i. e. not double, not lined nor faced, nor stuffed ]. (TA in art. سمط .) -b2- See إِزَازٌ .
لحف.summary_en: The root لحف (lHf) primarily refers to a covering or wrapper, especially a night-wrapper or a garment that is not lined or stuffed.

لحن.definition_en: لَحْنٌ ذ The meaning of speech; its intended sense or import: (S, K, TA:) its intent: (TA:) [it is direct: and also indirect: ] an indication thereof whereby the person addressed is made to understand one's intent; so says Az (Msb: [and the like is said in the TA on the authority of AHeyth:]) an oblique, or ambiguous, mode of speech: (Msb:) an inclining of speech to obliqueness, or ambiguity, and equivocal allusion. (Bd, in xlvii. 32.) -b2- A barbarism, an incorrect word. -b3- عَرَفْتُهُ فِى لَحْنِ كَلَامِهِ and فى نَحْوِ كلامه . and فى مِعْرَاضِ كلامه signify the same. (Msb in art. عرض .) See the last of these voce عَرُوضٌ . -b4- لَحْنٌ A modulated sound; expl. as being مِنَ الأَصْوَاتِ المَصُوغَةِ المَوْضُوعَةِ : pl. أَلْحَانٌ and لُحُونٌ . (K.) You say, أَلْحَانُ الأَغَانِى [ The modula
لحن.summary_en: The root لحن (lHn) refers to the meaning or intent of speech, often implying an indirect or ambiguous sense. It can also denote a grammatical error or barbarism in language.

لحي.summary_en: The root لحي (lHy) primarily refers to the jawbone and, by extension, the beard. It can also describe the act of shaving or trimming the beard, or striking someone on the jaw.

لزب.definition_en: لَازِبٌ ذ Adhering, or adhesive, or cohesive, clay or mud. (S.) -b2- Being, or remaining, fixed, settled, firm, or constant. (S.) -b3- صَارَ الشَّىْءُ ضَرْبَةَ لَازِبٍ The thing became fixed, settled, firm, or constant, (S, K,) and severe: (TA:) [or, a constant infliction: ] or, indispensable, or necessary: i. e., the blow of a sword that sticks, or remains fixed, [ in the wound ]. (Aboo-Bekr, cited in the TA.) لازب is here the same as لازم : (K:) the latter is the original word; the م being changed into ب ; and is also used in this phrase: (TA:) but لازب , in this in- stance, is the more chaste. (S.) En-Nábighah says, فَلَا تَحْسِبُونَ الخَيْرَ لَا شَرَّ بَعْدَهُ وَلَا تَحْسِبُونَ الشَّرَّ ضَرْبَةَ لَازِبِ [ Then think ye not that good shall have no evil after it, nor think ye that evil s
لزب.summary_en: The root لزب (lazib) describes something adhering, fixed, or constant, like cohesive clay. It can also mean indispensable or necessary.

لظي.summary_en: The root لظي (lzhy) primarily refers to a blazing, intensely burning fire, specifically the flame without smoke.

لفح.definition_en: 1 لَفَحَتْهُ النَّارُ ذ , (S, M, K,) aor. لَفَحَ , (M,) inf. n. لَفْحٌ and لَفَحَانٌ , (M, K,) The fire smote, or hurt, his face; as also لفحت وَجْهَهُ : (M, O:) the fire burned him; (TA;) as also نفحته النار بِحَرِّهَا ; (S, K;) and in like manner the hot wind called سَمُوم : (S:) لَفَحَتْ and نَفَحَتْ are syn., except that the effect of النَّفْح is greater than that of اللَّفْح : (Zj:) or لَفْحٌ relates to a hot wind; and نَفْحٌ , to a cold, or cool, wind: (As, S:) you also say لفحته السَّمُوم meaning the سموم blew in his face. (L.) -b2- Also لَفَحَهُ , aor. لَفَحَ , He smote, or struck, him, with a sword, (S, K,) lightly, or slightly: you say لَفَحْتُهُ بِالسَّيْفِ لَفْحَةً I struck him with the sword a light, or slight, blow. (S.)
لفح.summary_en: The root لفح (lfH) primarily describes fire or hot wind (سموم) striking or smiting a face, causing a burning sensation. It can also mean to strike lightly with a sword.

لفظ.definition_en: 1 لَفَظَهُ ذ , (S, M, Msb, K,) and لَفَظَ بِهِ , (M, K,) aor. لَفِظَ , (S, M, Msb, K,) inf. n. لَفْظٌ ; (T, S, M, Msb;) and لَفِظَهُ , and لَفِظَ بِهِ , aor. لَفِظَ ; (Ibn- Abbád, K.) but the former is that which is commonly known; (TA;) He ejected it; cast it forth; [ disgorged it; ] (T, S, M, Msb, K;) namely a thing that was in his mouth; (T;) his spittle, &c.; (Msb;) from ( مِنْ ) his mouth. (S, TA.) And لَفَظَ alone, [elliptically,] He ejected what had entered between his teeth, of food. (TA.) You say also, لَفَظَتِ الحَيَّةُ سَمَّهَا (tropical:) [ The serpent ejected its poison. ] (TA.) And لَفَظَ عَصْبَهُ , lit. He ejected his spittle that stuck and dried in his mouth; meaning (tropical:) he died; (T, TA;) as also لَفَظَ نَفْسَهُ , aor. لَفِظَ , inf. n. as above; (M, TA;) and لَفَظَ
لفظ.summary_en: The root لَفَظَ (lafaza) primarily means to eject or cast forth something from the mouth, like spittle or food.

لقب.definition_en: لَقَبٌ ذ A by-name; a surname; a nickname; syn. نَبَزٌ : (S, K:) a name of reproach; an opprobrious appellation: in this sense forbidden: (Msb:) it is said in the Kur, xlix., 11, لَا تَنَابَزُوا بِٱلْأَلْقَابِ Call not one another by nicknames; i. e., let not one of you call another by a name which he dislikes: (Jel:) also a by-name, or surname, which is not one of reproach: such are the surnames given to certain Imáms; الأَنْمَشُ and الأَخْفَشُ and الاَّعْرَجُ and the like; and such are not forbidden, because by them is not meant reproach, or disparagement, but they are meant as mere appellations given with the approval of the persons to whom they are applied. (Msb.) [A لَقَب is distinguished from a كُنْيَة , q. v.] Pl. أَلْقَابٌ . (S, K.) -b2- [ لَقَبُ ٱسْمٍ The appellation given to a no
لقب.summary_en: A *laqab* is a by-name, surname, or nickname. It can be a name of reproach, which is forbidden in the Quran (49:11).

لقح.definition_en: 4 القح الفَحْلُ النَّاقَةَ ذ , (S, Msb,) inf. n. إِلْقَاحٌ ; (Msb;) and , (A,) [inf. n. تَلْقِيحٌ ;] The stallion-camel made the she-camel to conceive, or become pregnant; impregnated her; got her with young. (Msb.) -b2- القح النَّخْلَةَ , inf. n. إِلْقَاحٌ , [and quasi-inf. n. لَقَاحٌ , q. v.; et vide infra;] and , inf. n. تَلْقِيحٌ ; (S, Msb, A, K;) and , inf. n. لَقْحٌ ; (K;) (tropical:) He fecundated the palm-tree by means of the , or spadix of the male tree, which is bruised, or brayed, and sprinkled [ upon the spadix of the female ]: (A:) or, by inserting a stalk of a raceme of the male tree into the spathe [ of the female, after shaking off the pollen of the former upon the spadix of the female; for such is the general practice]: this is done in the following manner: you leave the s
لقح.summary_en: The root لقح (lqH) primarily means to impregnate or fecundate. It describes a stallion impregnating a she-camel or the artificial pollination of a palm tree.

لقم.definition_en: 1 لَقِمَ لُقْمَةً ذ , [aor. لَقَمَ ,] inf. n. لَقْمٌ , (JK, MS,) [ He gobbled a gobbet, or morsel, or mouthful, or] he swallowed the gobbet; and so . (S.) لَقِمَهُ , aor. لَقَمَ , (Msb, K, &c.,) inf. n. لَقْمٌ , (JK, MA, K, MS, JM,) or لَقَمٌ (Msb, [app. a mistake,]) He gobbled it; i. e., ate it quickly, (Msb, K, TA,) as also , (Msb, [see above,]) and hastily; drew it with his mouth, and ate it quickly. (TA) -b2- لَقِمَ بِيَدِهِ He put morsels into his mouth (i. e., his own mouth) with his hand. See an ex. voce رَقَعَ . This seems to be the primary signification.
لقم.summary_en: The root LQM primarily means to gobble or swallow a morsel quickly. It can also refer to putting morsels into one's mouth with the hand. The core idea is swift, often eager, consumption of food.

لهم.definition_en: 4 أَلْهَمَهُ ٱللّٰهُ لِلْخَيْرِ ذ [ God directed him by inspiration to that which was good, or to prosperity ] (TA, art. وفق ) -b2- أَلْهَمَهُ إِيَّاهُ He suggested it to him; (in the order of the words, he suggested to him it; ) he put it into his mind.
لهم.summary_en: The root لَهَمَ (lhm) primarily means for God to inspire or direct someone towards good or prosperity. It also signifies suggesting something to someone or putting an idea into their mind.

لوت.definition_en: 1 لَاتَ ذ , aor. يَلُوتُ , He told, narrated, or gave an account of, a thing different from that respecting which he was asked. (K.) [But accord. to some, the aor. is يَلِيتُ , and the verb belongs to art. ليت .] It was said to ElAsadeeyeh, “ What is المُدَاحَلَةُ ” and she answered أَنْ يَلِيتَ الإِنْسَانُ شَيْئًا قَدْ عَلِمَهُ , i. e., “ The concealing a thing that one knows, and telling, or narrating, something different from it. ” (TA.) -b2- لَاتَ الخَبَرَ , aor. يَلُوتُ , He concealed the news, or information, (K,) and related what was different therefrom. (TA.) [But see above.] -b3- لَاتَ الرَّجُلَ , aor. يَلُوتُ , He told the man, or narrated to him, a thing in a manner different from the real state of the case: or he expressed the news, or information, to him obscurely, or enigmati
لوت.summary_en: The root لوت (lwt) primarily means to distort or conceal information, telling something different from the truth. It can also mean to withhold or restrain someone from their path.

لوذ.definition_en: 1 لَاذَ بِهِ ذ , aor. يَلُوذُ , (S, A, L, Msb,) inf. n. لَوْذٌ (S, L, K) and لِيَاذٌ (S, A, L, K) and لِوَاذٌ (L, Msb, K) and لَوَاذٌ and لُوَاذٌ , (Msb, K,) He had recourse to it, (a mountain [&c.], Msb,) or him, for refuge or protection or preservation; (S, A, L, Msb;) as also به , inf. n. لِوَاذٌ ; (A;) and به ; (Msb;) sought, or took, refuge in it, or him; (S, L;) and joined himself to him; and sought, desired, implored, or called for, aid, or succour, of him: (L:) he protected, concealed, defended, or fortified himself by it, (L, K,) or him; (L;) as also به , (L,) inf. n. مُلَاوَذَةٌ (L, K) and لِوَاذٌ ; (L;) and . (L.) -b2- لَاذَ بِهِ , (L,) inf. n. as above, in the commencement of the art., (K,) It encompassed, or surrounded, it; (L, K; *) as also , (L,) inf. n. إِلَاذَةٌ . (L, K.)
لوذ.summary_en: The root "lwdh" primarily means to seek refuge, protection, or aid from someone or something. It also describes something encompassing or surrounding another, like a road around a house.

ليت.definition_en: 1 لَاتَهُ ذ , aor. يَليِتُ , (inf. n. لَيْتٌ , S,) as also لَاتَةُ , aor. يَلُوتُ ; or لَاتَهُ عَنْ وَجْهِهِ ; as also ; (and أَلَتَهُ ; S, K, art. أَلت ;) He withheld him, or restrained him, and turned him, or averted him, from his course, purpose, or object. (S, K.) The Rájiz says, وَلَيْلَةٍ ذَاتِ دُجًا سَرَيْتُ وَلَمْ يَلِتْنِى عَنْ سُرَاهَا لَيْتُ [ During many a dark night (or many a rainy, or wet, night, accord. to the reading in the TA, namely ذات نَدًى ) have I journeyed; ] and no hindrance hindered me from journeying during it: (S:) or the meaning is -b2- and no averting thing averted me, &c.; لَيْتُ being put for لَائِتُ : or, nothing made me to repent, and say, لَيْتَنِى مَا سَرَيْتُهَا Would that I had not journeyed during it! or, no deficiency, nor any impotence, averted me,
ليت.summary_en: The root "lyt" primarily means to withhold, restrain, or diminish something. In the Quran, it appears in the sense of not diminishing one's reward for deeds.

مجس.definition_en: المَجُوسُ ذ [ The Magians ]; a certain nation: it is a Persian word: (Msb:) مَجُوسُ [here written in two copies of the S and in the CK with tenween, but afterwards shown in the S to be imperfectly decl., and expressly said to be so in the Msb, art. هود ,] is pl. of مَجُوسِىٌّ : (S, K:) [or rather the former is a coll. gen. n., and the latter is the n. un.:] the latter is a rel. n. from المَجُوسِيَّةُ , q. v., (S,) and is an epithet applied to a man: (K:) المَجُوسُ has the article ال only because it is used as a pl., (Aboo-'Alee, S, M, L,) for المَجُوسِيُّونَ ; (ISd, L;) for otherwise مجوس could not receive the art., being of itself determinate; and it is also of the fem. gender; wherefore, with respect to inflection, it is like the قَبِيلَة , not the حَىّ ; and the same is the case with re
مجس.summary_en: The root مجس (mjs) refers to the Magians, a Persian nation. The word "Majus" is Persian in origin, meaning "short ear," and is an arabicized term.

محل.definition_en: 5 تَمَحَّلْتُ مَالًا بِغَيْرِ ثَمَنٍ ذ I laboured to acquire property without price: (Msb:) or, accord. to Az, تَمَحَّلَ مَالًا means he laboured, and exercised art or management, in seeking [ to acquire ] property. (TA.) See also تَعَلَّثَ .
محل.summary_en: The root مَحَلَ (maḥala) implies striving or laboring to acquire something, often property, possibly without direct payment or through skillful management. It suggests an effortful pursuit.

مخض.definition_en: 1 مَخَضَ اللَّبَنَ ذ , (S, A, Mgh, Msb, K,) aor. مَخُضَ , (S, A, Msb, K,) and مَخِضَ , and مَخَضَ , (S, Msb, K,) inf. n. مَخْضٌ , (Msb,) He churned, or beat and agitated, the milk, (Mgh,) in the مِمْخَضَة : (A, Mgh:) and (A) he took the butter of the milk: (A, K:) or he extracted, or fetched out, the butter of the milk, by putting water in it, and agitating it: (Msb:) or مَخْضٌ signifies one's agitating the مِمْخَض wherein is the milk of which the butter has been taken. (Lth.) -b2- [Hence,] مَخْضٌ relates also to many things. (TA.) Thus, you say, (TA) مَخَضَ الشَّىْءَ , (K, TA,) inf. n. as above, (TA,) (assumed tropical:) He shook, or agitated, the thing vehemently. (K, TA.) It is said in a trad., مُرَّ عَلَيْهِ بِجِنَازَةٍ تُمْخَضُ مَخْضًا ; (L;) or تُمْخَضُ مَخْضَ الزّقّ ; (O;) (assumed
مخض.summary_en: The root "mkhD" primarily means to churn milk, extracting butter by agitation. It extends to shaking or agitating anything vehemently, like a corpse on a bier or water in a well.

مرو.summary_en: The root مرو (mrw) primarily refers to the act of rubbing or passing the hand over something, often to extract or obtain something.

مزن.definition_en: مُزْنٌ ذ Clouds (K, and Ham, p. 564) of any kind: (Ham, ibid:) or white clouds: (S, K, and Ham, p. 53:) or clouds containing water. (K.)
مزن.summary_en: The root "mzn" refers to clouds. It can mean clouds of any kind, specifically white clouds, or clouds that are heavy with water.

مسخ.definition_en: مَسْخٌ ذ and , (L, K,) [the former originally an inf. n., and therefore used as sing. and dual and pl. without alteration, though مُسُوخٌ is used as a pl. by late writers, (see De Sacy's Chrest. Ar., ii. 273,)] the latter of the measure فَعِيلٌ in the sense of the measure مَفْعُولٌ , (L,) Transformed, or metamorphosed, into a worse, or more foul, or more ugly, shape. (L, K.) Ex. الجَانُّ مَسْخُ الجِنِّ The Jánn, which are slender serpents, are the transformed of the Jinn, or Genii; like as certain persons of the Children of Israel were transformed into apes. [See Kur, ii. 61.] (L, from a trad.) -b2- Also, the ↓ latter, Deformed; rendered ugly in make, or form. (K.) Hence, some say, the appellation of الدَّجَّالُ [more commonly المَسِيحُ الدّجّان , q. v.]. (TA.) -b3- Also, the same, (tropic
مسخ.summary_en: The root "mskh" refers to being transformed into a worse or uglier shape, like humans into apes. It can also describe something deformed, or a person lacking beauty, being weak, or stupid.

مسد.definition_en: 1 مَسَدَ ذ , aor. مَسُدَ , (S, M, L,) inf. n. مَسْدٌ , (S, M, L, K,) He twisted a rope: (M, L, K:) or he twisted it well. (ISk, S, L.) -b2- مَسَدَ , aor. مَسُدَ , (M, L,) inf. n. مَسْدٌ , (S, M, L, K,) He pursued a journey laboriously, or with energy; or he held on, or continued, the journey; syn. أَدْأَبَ السَّيْرَ , (S, M, L, K,) by night: (S, M, L:) or he journeyed on continually, whether by night or by day: (M, L:) because the so journeying renders an animal lean, or lank. (Lth, L.) -b3- مَسَدَ , aor. مَسُدَ , [inf. n. مَسْدٌ ,] (tropical:) It (leguminous herbage, A, or continued travel, Lth) rendered an animal lean, lank, light of flesh, slender, or lank in the belly. (Lth, A, L.) El-'Abdee says, describing a she-camel, and likening her to a wild bull, يَمْسُدُهُ القَفْرُ وَلَيْلٌ سَد
مسد.summary_en: The root مَسَدَ (m-s-d) primarily means "to twist a rope well." It also describes arduous, continuous travel that makes an animal lean.

مسو.summary_en: The root مَسَّ (massa) primarily means to touch, feel, or handle something. It can also imply affecting or befalling someone, often in a negative sense like affliction or madness.

مشج.definition_en: مَشِيجٌ ذ (S, K) and (K) and like كتف in its two dial. forms, ( فِى لُغَتَيْهِ , K accord. to the TA,) i. e., and , or like كَتِفٌ in a dial. of little authority, ( فِى لُغَيَّةٍ , CK and a MS. copy of the K.) [See مَشْيُوحَآءُ , in art. شيج .] A thing mixed, or confused: (S, K:) or any two things mixed together: or any two colours mixed together: or what is a mixture of red and white: (TA:) pl. (of all the above forms, TA) أَمْشَاجٌ . (S, K.) -b2- نُطْفَةٌ أَمْشَاجٌ , occurring in the Kur, lxxvi. 2, [ A drop consisting of ] mixtures; meaning the sperma genitale, because it is a mixture of various kinds: (ISk:) or [ a drop consisting of ] mixtures of sperma genitale and blood: (Zj:) or [ a drop consisting of ] the sperma genitale of the man mixed with that of the woman and with her blood.
مشج.summary_en: The root مَشِيجٌ (mashīj) refers to something mixed or confused, like two colors or substances blended.

مطو.summary_en: The root مَطَوَ (maṭawa) primarily means to stretch, extend, or prolong something.

معز.definition_en: مَعْزٌ ذ , and , (S, A, Msb, K,) gen. ns., (S, Msb,) [or rather quasi-pl. ns., signifying Goats; ] the kind of غَنَم opposed to ضَأْنٌ ; (S, A, K;) the kind of عَنَم that have hair; (Msb, TA;) the ضأْن being those that have wool; (TA;) as also , (S, Msb, K,) accord. to Sb, (S,) with tenween, (S, Msb,) when indeterminate, (Msb,) and perfectly decl., (S,) the ا [which is written ى ] being a letter of quasicoördination, not a characteristic of the fem. gender, (S, Msb,) for the word is quasi-coördinate to دِرْهَمٌ , of the measure فِعْلَلٌ ; for the ا of quasi-coördination follows the same rules as a letter belonging to the word itself, as is shown by their saying and أُرَيْطٍ [originally مُعَيْزِىٌ and أُرَيْطِىٌ ] as the dim. forms of مِعْزًى and أَرْطًى with tenween, the letter next after
معز.summary_en: The root م-ع-ز (m-ʿ-z) primarily refers to "goats." It denotes the hairy kind of sheep, as opposed to the woolly kind.

معي.summary_en: The root م-ع-ي primarily signifies being with someone or something, indicating companionship, presence, or association. It can also denote support or agreement, implying a shared state or action.

مكو.definition_en: مُكَّآءٌ ذ The bird so called, because of its colour: see أَخْرَجُ .
مكو.summary_en: The root مكو (mkw) refers to a bird, likely named for its color. The definition points to "أَخْرَجُ" for further details on the color.

مير.definition_en: مِيرَةٌ ذ : i. q. طَعَامٌ [here meaning Wheat, or other corn, and food, victuals, or provision, of any kind,] (T, S, A, * Mgh, Msb, K, *) which a man brings, or conveys or purveys ( يَمْتَارُهُ ) [ to be laid up in store for himself or his family or household, or for sale ]; (S, TA;) and signifies the same as مِيرَةٌ [in these senses, as will be seen from what follows], and is applied to victuals, or food, or aliment, syn. قُوتٌ . (TA.) Ex. جَالِبُ المِيرَةِ , (K,) or , (M,) [ The bringer, or conveyer, or purveyor, of wheat, &c.] And أَتَاهُ بِمِيرَةٍ He brought him طَعَام . (T.) And جَاؤُوا بِٱلْمِيرَةِ [ They brought, or conveyed, or purveyed, the wheat, &c.] (A.) And خَيْرٌ وَلَا مَا عِنْدَهُ [ He has not wealth, nor wheat, &c.] (T, S, A.) -b2- Also, (T,) The bringing, or conveying, or
مير.summary_en: The root مير (myr) primarily refers to provisions, like wheat or other food, that a person gathers or conveys for storage or sale.

نبز.definition_en: 1 نَبَزَهُ ذ , aor. نَبِزَ , inf. n. نَبْزٌ , He called him, or named him, by a by-name, surname, or nickname; he by-named him, surnamed him, or nicknamed him; syn. لَقَّبَهُ ; (S, Msb, K;) mostly signifying he called him, or named him, by a nickname, a name of reproach, or an opprobrious appellation; (TA;) as also : (K:) or the latter is with teshdeed to denote muchness, or frequency, or repetition, of the action, or its application to many objects: you say, فُلَانٌ بِالصَّبْيَانِ Such a one by-names, surnames, or [rather] nicknames, the children; syn. يُلَقِّبْهُمْ . (S.) -b2- النَّبْزُ is also syn. with اللَّمْزُ ; (K;) or is like the latter: (TA;) [i. e., نَبَزَهُ also signifies He upbraided, or reproached, him; or the like. ]
نبز.summary_en: The root نبز (nbz) primarily means to call someone by a by-name, surname, or nickname, often implying a name of reproach or an opprobrious appellation. It can also mean to upbraid or reproach someone.

نبط.definition_en: نَبَطٌ ذ What first appears of the water of a well (IDrd, K) when it is dug; (IDrd;) as also : (K:) or the water that comes forth from a well when it is first dug: (Zj:) or the water that issues forth from the bottom of a well when it is dug; (S, accord. to one copy;) or this is termed : (S, accord. to another copy; and TA:) pl. [of pauc.] أَنْبَاطٌ and [of mult.] نُبُوطٌ . (TA.) -b2- [Hence the saying,] فُلَانٌ قَرِيبُ الثَّرَى بَعِيدُ النَّبَطِ (assumed tropical:) Such a one's promising is near, [but] his fulfilling is remote: i. e. he promises, but does not fulfil. (IAar.) And فلان لَا يُدْرَكُ نَبَطُهُ , (TA,) and لَا يُدْرَكُ لَهُ نَبَطٌ , (ISd, TA,) (tropical:) Such a one's depth is not known, (K, * TA,) and the extent of his knowledge: (TA:) or such a one's depth is not known; meani
نبط.summary_en: The root نبط (nbT) primarily refers to the first water that appears when digging a well, or water that oozes from a mountain. It can also describe a well whose water was obtained through labor.

نتق.definition_en: فُنْتُقٌ ذ A خَان of the way or road; (Ibn-'Abbád, O, K;) a dial. var. of فُنْدُقٌ [q. v.]; disapproved by El-Khafájee; but heard by Fr from a desertArab of Kudá'ah, as meaning a فُنْدُق , i. e. خَان . (TA.)
نتق.summary_en: The root نتق (ntq) refers to a "khān" or inn, a resting place along a road. It's a dialectal variant of فندق (funduq), though some scholars disapproved of its use.

نجد.definition_en: نَجْدٌ ذ High, or elevated, land or country: (S, L, Msb, K:) or hard, and rugged, and elevated, or high, table-land: only stony and rugged, or hard, elevated land, like a mountain, standing over against one and intercepting his view of what is behind it, but not very high, is thus called: (L:) pl. أَنْجُدٌ , (S, L, K,) a pl. of pauc., (TA,) and أَنْجَادٌ , (L, K,) [also a pl. of pauc.,] and نِجَادٌ and نُجُودٌ (S. L, K) and نُجُدٌ ; (IAar, L, K;) and pl. of نُجُودٌ , أَنْجِدَةٌ ; [another pl. of pauc.;] (S, K;) or this is a mistake, and it is pl. of نِجَادٌ , like as أَحْمِرَةٌ is pl. of حِمَارٌ ; or it is a pl. deviating from common rule. (IB, L.) You say أُعْلُ هَاتِيكَ النِّجَادَ Ascend thou these high lands; and هَاذَاكَ النِّجَادَ this high land, making it singular. (L.) -b2- نَجْدٌ ,
نجد.summary_en: The root نجد (njd) primarily refers to high, elevated land, often rugged. It also denotes an elevated, conspicuous road or path.

نجس.definition_en: نَجِسٌ ذ and (S, A, O, Msb, K) and and and (A, O, K) Unclean, dirty, filthy, or impure: (A, Msb, K:) the last of these is only used when the word is preceded or followed by رِجْسٌ : (ISd:) or only when it is preceded by رِجْسٌ : (Fr, S, * and Har in the Durrat-el-Ghowwás:) but this remark correctly applies only to the greater number of instances: (M, F:) the second of the above five forms is an inf. n. used as an epithet; (A, Msb;) and is [therefore] used as sing. and dual. and pl. and masc. and fem., without variation: (TA:) [and the first and last are also used as pls., as will be seen below; but when so used, I suppose قَوْمٌ or some other coll. gen. n. to be understood:] the pl. [of every one of the other forms] is أَنْجَاسٌ (A, Msb, TA) and [of the fourth and fifth, and perhaps of the
نجس.summary_en: The root نجس (njs) describes something as unclean, dirty, or impure. It can refer to physical impurity or, tropically, to spiritual impurity, as in the Quranic verse about polytheists.

نحب.definition_en: نَحْبٌ ذ A vow. (S, K.) -b2- قَضَى نَحْبَهُ He died: or he was slain in an expedition undertaken for the sake of God's religion: originally meaning he accomplished his vow: see Kur, xxxiii. 23: (Msb:) as though he had constrained himself [by a vow] to fight until he died: (TA:) or it signifies he ended his term, or period of life; ended his days: (Fr, Zj:) [or he finished his time: (as implied in the S): or he yielded his soul: or he accomplished his want: ] from significations given below. (TA.) -b3- نَحْبٌ A great bet, or wager: syn. خَطَرٌ عَظِيمٌ . (K.) So in the following verse of Jereer: بِطِخْفَةَ جَالَدْنَا المُلُوكَ وَخَيْلُنَا عَشِيَّةَ بِسْطَامٍ جَرَيْنَ عَلَى نَحْبِ [ In Tikhfeh we contended with the sword with the kings; and our horses, in the evening of Bistám, ran for a grea
نحب.summary_en: The root نَحْب (naḥb) primarily means a vow or an oath. It is famously used in the phrase قَضَى نَحْبَهُ, meaning "he fulfilled his vow" or "he died," often in battle for God's cause.

نحر.definition_en: 1 نَحَرَ ذ , (A, Msb, K,) aor. نَحَرَ , (Msb, K,) inf. n. نَحْرٌ (S, Msb, K) and مَنْحَرٌ (Msb) and تَنْحَارٌ [an intensive form], (K,) He stabbed, or stuck, (A, K,) a camel, (A, TA,) or a beast, (Msb,) [but generally the former,] in his نَحْر , (A,) or in his مَنْحَر , (TA,) where the windpipe ( حُلْقُوم ) commences in the uppermost part of the breast; (K, TA;) [i. e., in the لَبَّة ; for] نَحْرٌ in the لَبَّة is like ذَبْحٌ in the throat. (S.) [Hence,] يَوْمٌ النَّحْرِ [ The day of the stabbing of the camels &c. ]; (K;) and عِيدُ النَّحْرِ [ the festival of the stabbing of the camels &c.]; (Msb;) the tenth of [ the month ] Dhu-l-Hijjeh; (K;) because then the camels and cows and bulls brought as offerings to Mekkeh, for sacrifice, are stabbed. (TA.) -b2- He slew. (TA.) -b3- نَحَرَهُ , aor
نحر.summary_en: The root نَحَرَ primarily means to slaughter a camel by stabbing its throat. It also extends to hitting the throat area or facing something directly.

نخر.definition_en: 1 نَخَرَ ذ , (S, A, Msb, K,) aor. نَخُرَ (S, Msb, K) and نَخِرَ , (S, K,) inf. n. نَخِيرٌ , (S, A, Msb, K,) and نَخْرٌ , (CK, but omitted in MS. copies of the K,) said of a horse, (As, TA,) and of an ass, (A, TA,) and of a man, (TA,) [ He snorted; and he snored; ] he made a sound, or noise, with the nose; (S;) he made a sound, or noise, from his nose; he prolonged the breath from the خَيَاشِيم [or air-passages of the nose ]; (Msb;) he made a sound, or noise, from his خياشيم , as though it were a musical note issuing convulsively; (TA;) he uttered a prolonged sound, or noise, from his خياشيم : (A, K:) the sound which horses make, termed نَخِيرٌ , is from the nostrils; that termed شَخِيرٌ , from the mouth; and that termed كَرِيرٌ , from the chest. (As, in TA, art. شخر .) You also say of a wo
نخر.summary_en: The root نخر (nkhr) primarily describes making a sound from the nose, like snorting or snoring. It also refers to something, especially bone or wood, becoming old, wasted, and crumbling.

نسر.definition_en: نَسْرٌ ذ (S, M, Msb, K, &c.) and sometimes [agreeably with the modern general pronunciation] and , (Sheykh-el-Islám Zekereeyà, in his Comm. on the Expos. of Bd,) but this is very strange, (MF,) [The vulture; app. any vulture, whatever be its species or variety, known to the Arabs, except the رَخَم , or aquiline vulture; and said to be applied by some of the Arabs to the eagle; (see also نُسَارِيَّةٌ ;) agreeing with the Hebrew נֶשֶׁר , which is plainly applied to the former bird in Micah, i. 16, and probably in other instances;] a certain bird, (S, M, A, Msb, K,) well known; (A, Msb;) so called because it plucks ( يَنْسُِرُ ) a thing, and swallows it, (A, and so in some copies of the K,) or, and pulls it out (so in some copies of the K,) or, and chases and captures it; (so in some copies o
نسر.summary_en: The root نسر (nasr) primarily refers to the vulture, a well-known bird of prey.

نضج.definition_en: 4 انضج ذ He rendered fruit, or flesh-meat, (whether dried in the sun or roasted, TA, [or cooked in any way],) perfectly fit for being used, or for being eaten: rendered ripe, or mature: thoroughly cooked: (S, K:) it (the proper time) rendered fruit ripe, or mature; ripened, or matured it. (TA.) -b2- AHn uses this verb in a strange manner, explaining the expression نَبَاتٌ مَهْرُوْءٌ by the words أَلَّذِى قَدْ أَنْضَجَهُ البَرْدُ [meaning, a plant, or herbage, that is nipped, shrunk, shrivelled, or blasted, by the cold ]: this is strange because إِنْضَاج is an effect of heat; not of cold. (M.) [See أَحْرَقَ .] -b3- أَنْضِجْ رَأْيَكَ (tropical:) Mature thy judgment, or thine opinion ]. (A.) -b4- لَا يُنْضِجُ السكُرَاعَ , (L,) or لَا كُرَاعًا , (A,) [ He does not thoroughly cook the slender p
نضج.summary_en: The root نضج (nDj) primarily means to ripen or mature fruit, or to thoroughly cook meat, making it fit for consumption. It can also be used metaphorically for maturing judgment or opinion.

نضخ.definition_en: 1 نَضَخَهُ ذ , aor. نَضَحَ , [and نَضِحَ , see below], inf. n. نَضْخٌ , He sprinkled him, or it, [with water &c.]: or i. q. نَضَحَهُ : (K:) AZ says, نَضْخٌ signifies the act of sprinkling, like نَضْحٌ ; these two words being syn.: you say نَضَخْتُ , aor. أَنْضَخُ : (S:) or the former signifies less than the latter: (K:) so most say: (L:) or the former signifies what is unintentional; and the latter, what is intentional: (IAar, L:) As says, that the latter is the act of man: (L:) and the former, he says, signifies more than the latter, and has no pret. nor aor.: and Aboo-'Othmán Et-Towwazee says, that the former signifies the mark, or effect, that remains upon a garment or other thing, and that the act is termed نَضْحٌ , with ح unpointed: (S:) As says, that نَضْخٌ has no verb nor act. part.
نضخ.summary_en: The root نضخ (nDkh) means to sprinkle, often with water. It's similar to نضح (ndH), but can imply a less intentional or more forceful sprinkling.

نطح.definition_en: نَطِيحٌ ذ Smitten with the horn and so killed: fem. with ة : (L, K:) pl. نَطْحَى (L) and نَطَائِحُ : (Lh, L:) you say also نَعْجَةٌ نَطِيحٌ , as well as نطيحة . (L.) -b2- Also نَطِيحَةٌ A sheep or goat (Az) smitten with the horn and so killed. (Az, S,) and of which the flesh is therefore unlawful to be eaten: (Az:) the ة is added because it is made a subst., not an epithet: (Az:) or because the quality of a subst. predominates in it, as in the case of فَرِيسَةٌ and أَكِيلَةٌ and رَمِيَّةٌ . (S.) -b3- نَطِيحٌ and (tropical:) What comes to thee from before thee, (or faces thee, L,) of birds and wild animals (S, L, K) &c., which one rouses by throwing a stone or by crying out, and from the course of which one augurs evil or good: (L:) contr. of قَعِيدٌ . (S.) [The نطيح or is of evil omen.] -b
نطح.summary_en: Naṭīḥah refers to an animal, often a sheep or goat, that has been killed by being gored by another animal's horn, making its meat unlawful to eat.

نعق.definition_en: 1 نَعَقَ بِهِمْ إِلَى الفِتْنَةٍ ذ He hallooed them on to fight, etc.: see 10 in art. عو .
نعق.summary_en: The root نعق (naʿaqa) primarily means to cry out or shout. It can describe a shepherd calling his flock or, metaphorically, inciting people to conflict or discord.

نعل.definition_en: نَعْلٌ ذ [ A sandal: a sole: ] the thing by which the foot is preserved, or protected, from the ground; (K;) syn. حِذَآءٌ : and also applied to a تَاسُومَة [or shoe ]. (Msb.) What is now called تَاسُومَة . (IAth, TA.) It often signifies only a sole: so in the S, K, Msb, &c., in art. خصف &c. -b2- The leathern shoe, or sandal, of a camel; which is attached by thongs, or straps, called سَرَائِح (pl. of سَرِيحَةٌ ) to the خَدَمَة or plaited thong which surrounds the pastern: see سَرِيحَةٌ and خَدَنَةٌ . -b3- نَعْلٌ of a sword The iron, (Kr, S, K,) or silver, (S,) thing [or shoe ] at the lower end of the scabbard. (Kr, S, K.) See غَاشِيَةٌ , and 2 in art. فرص . -b4- نَعْلٌ meaning A حَرَّة , or hard rugged tract of land, &c.: see رَحْلٌ . -b5- نَعْلٌ (tropical:) A wife. See عَتَبَة .
نعل.summary_en: The root نعل (naʿl) primarily refers to a sandal or shoe, protecting the foot from the ground.

نغض.definition_en: 1 نَغَضَ ذ , aor. نَغِضَ , (Ks, S, A, Msb, K,) and نَغُضَ , (S, A, K,) inf. n. نَغْضٌ (S, Msb, K) and نُغُوضٌ (S, K,) and نَغَضَانٌ and نَغَضٌ , (K,) It was or became, in a state of motion, commotion, agitation, or convulsion; it shook; shook about; wabbled; tottered; wagged; nodded; syn. تَحَرَّكَ , (S, A, Msb, K,) and اِضْطَرَبَ , (A, K,) فِى ٱرْتِجَافٍ ; (TA;) as also (Msb, K) and : (K:) it is said of a man's head; (S, TA;) and also, (S, A,) with نَغْضٌ and نَغَضَانٌ for its inf. ns., (S,) of a camel's saddle, (S, A,) and of the central incisor (S, TA) of a child, (S,) or of any tooth, as also ↓ the last of the verbs above mentioned; (A;) and of other things; (Msb, TA;) نَغْضٌ signifying any moving in a shaking or tremulous or convulsive manner ( فِى ٱرْتِجَافٍ ); (S, TA;) and نَغَضَتْ
نغض.summary_en: The root نغض (ngD) primarily describes motion, agitation, or shaking, like a wobbling head or a loose tooth. It can also mean to be disquieted or to rise and hasten towards an enemy.

نفث.definition_en: 1 نَفَثَ ذ , aor. نَفِثَ and نَفُثَ , inf. n. نَفْثٌ (S, K) and نَفَثَانٌ , (TA,) [ He puffed; or blew, without spitting: or he sputtered, or blew forth a little spittle in minute scattered particles: or] he spat: or he [ did as though he ] spat without ejecting spittle: and نَفَثَ فى العُقْدَةِ signifies he spat, ejecting a little spittle, upon the knot, in enchantment: (Msb:) or النَّفْثُ is like النَّفْخُ , or blowing, and less than التَّفْلُ , or spitting, or ejecting spittle from the mouth: (S, K:) or like blowing, with [ the emission of ] spittle: (Keshsháf;) or like blowing, as done in enchantment, without spittle: the action, if accompanied by spittle, being termed التفل : this is the most correct explanation: ('Ináyeh:) or gentle blowing without spittle: ( الاذكار :) or more than
نفث.summary_en: The root نفث (nfth) primarily means to puff or blow, often with a small amount of spittle, less than full spitting. It's used for enchantment, where one blows on knots.

نفح.definition_en: 1 نَفَحَ ذ , aor. تَفَحَ , (S, L, K,) inf. n. نَفْحٌ (L, K) and نُفُوحٌ (L) and نُفَاحٌ and نَفَحَانٌ , (K,) It (perfume) diffused its odour. (S, L, K.) -b2- نَفَحَتِ الرِّيحُ , (S, Msb, K,) aor. تَفُحَ , inf. n. نَفْحٌ , (Msb,) (tropical:) The wind blew: (S, Msb, K:) or blew gently; began to be in a state of commotion: (A:) نَفَحَتْ and لَفَحَتْ are syn., except that the effect of النَّفْح is greater than that of اللَّفْح : (Zj:) or, accord. to As, (S,) or IAar, (TA,) نَفْحٌ relates to a cold, or cool, wind; and لَفْحٌ , to a hot wind: (S, TA.) [but see نَفْحَةٌ ]. -b3- نَفَحَتْهُ الجنوب بِبَرْدِهَا (tropical:) The south wind blew upon it with its cold, or coolness. (IB.) -b4- نَفَحَ العِرْقَ , (aor. تَفَحَ , inf. n. نَفْحٌ , S,) (tropical:) The vein ejected, or spirted forth, blood. (S,
نفح.summary_en: The root "nfH" describes diffusion, like perfume spreading or wind blowing. It also refers to a vein spurting blood or a horse kicking. Figuratively, it means to give a gift or strike lightly.

نفي.summary_en: The root نفي primarily means to banish, exile, or send away. It also conveys the sense of denying, negating, or rejecting something, making it non-existent or invalid.

نقع.definition_en: 1 نَقَعَ ذ and It (water) remained, or stagnated, or collected, in a hollow, or cavity: (Mgh:) or remained long, and became altered: (Msb:) or the former [and latter] collected in a مَنْقَع : (S:) or the latter [and former] became yellow and altered. (K.) -b2- نَقَعَ and [ He macerated, steeped, or soaked, a medicine, in water, ] he left it in water until its colour became changed. (Msb.)
نقع.summary_en: The root نقع (nqE) primarily describes water that collects and stagnates, often changing in color or quality.

نكد.definition_en: 1 نَكِدَ ذ , aor. نَكَدَ , (S, K, &c.,) inf. n. نَكَدٌ , (S, L, Msb,) It (a man's life) was, or became, hard, or strait, and difficult. (S, L, Msb, K.) -b2- نَكِدَ It (a she-camel's milk) became deficient. (R.) -b3- نَكِدَ It (water) became exhausted. (A.) -b4- نَكِدَتِ الرَّكِيَّةُ The well came to have little water. (S, L, K.) -b5- نَكِدَ , aor. نَكَدَ , inf. n. نَكَدٌ , He was, or became, unpropitious, and mean: (L:) he became hard, or difficult: (Msb:) he gave little: or gave not at all: you say also نَكِدَ بِحَاجَتِنَا he was niggardly of that which we wanted. (L.) -b6- نَكَدَ حَاجَتَهُ , aor. نَكُدَ ; (K;) or نَكَدَهُ حَاجَتَهُ , (L,) He withheld from him, or refused him, his want. (L, K.) -b7- نَكَدَهُ , aor. نَكُدَ , He withheld from him, or refused him, what he asked: or [in the C
نكد.summary_en: The root نكد (nkd) describes hardship, scarcity, and difficulty. It refers to a hard life, deficient milk, or exhausted water.

نمرق.summary_en: The root نمرق (nmrq) primarily refers to a cushion or pillow, often used for reclining. It denotes a soft, elevated support for comfort, especially in a sitting or lying posture.

نمم.summary_en: The root نَمَم primarily refers to carrying tales or slander, especially with the intent to cause mischief or discord between people.

نهج.definition_en: 1 نَهَجَ ذ , (K, Msb,) aor. نَهَجَ , inf. n. نُهُوجٌ ; (Msb;) and ; (S, K;) It (a road, or way, S and Msb, and an affair, TA,) became manifest, plainly apparent, or open; (S, K, Msb,) and so, with respect to a road, . (K.) -b2- نَهَجَ , (S, K, Msb,) and , (K, Msb,) He, or it, rendered (a road, S and Msb, and an affair, TA,) manifest, plainly apparent, or open: (S, K, Msb:) -b3- إِعْمَلْ عَلَى مَا نَهَجْتُهُ لَكَ Do according to that which I have made manifest to thee. (S.) -A2- نَهَجَ , aor. نَهَجَ , (inf. n. نَهْجٌ ; TA,) and , It (attrition, TA) wore out, or rendered worn out, a garment. (K.) -b2- نَهِجَ , aor. نَهَجَ ; (A 'Obeyd, S, K;) and نَهَجَ , (K,) but this is disallowed by A 'Obeyd, (S,) and نَهُجَ , and ; (K;) It (a garment) became old and worn out: (K:) or signifies it began to
نهج.summary_en: The root نَهَجَ (nahaja) primarily means for a road or affair to become clear or manifest. It also describes making something clear or following a path.

نوأ.definition_en: 1 نَآءَ ذ , aor. يَنُوْءُ , inf. n. نَوْءٌ (S, K) and تَنْوَآءٌ , (K,) He rose, or arose, with effort and difficulty. (S, K.) -b2- نَآءَ بِحِمْلِهِ He rose with his burden with effort and difficulty. (TA:) he rose with his burden oppressed by its weight. (S, K.) -b3- تَنُوْءُبِعَجِيزَتِهَا She rises with her buttocks oppressed by their weight: said of a woman. (S.) -b4- نَآءَ بِصَدْرِهِ He arose. [App. said originally, if not only, of a camel.] (TA.) -b5- نَاءَ بِهِ and , It (a burden) oppressed him by its weight, and bent him, or weighed him down. (S, K,) -b6- تَنُوْءُ بِهَا عَجِيزَتُهَا Her buttocks oppress her by their weight: said of a woman. (S.) -b7- نَآءَ He was oppressed by weight, (K,) and fell down: (S, K:) thus the verb bears two [partially] opposite significations. (K.) -b
نوأ.summary_en: The root نوأ (nwA) primarily describes rising or moving with effort, often burdened by weight. It can also mean to be weighed down or fall.

نوش.definition_en: 1 نَاشَهُ ذ , aor. يَنُوشُ , (S, A, Msb,) inf. n. نَوْشٌ , (S, A, Msb, K,) He took it, or reached it, absolutely, or with the hand, or with the extended hand; (S, A, Msb, K;) as also , (A, TA,) inf. n. تَنَاوُشٌ ; (S, Mgh, Msb, K;) and , (A, TA,) inf. n. إِنْتِيَاشٌ : (S, K:) it is also written with ء ; (A, K, TA, in art. نأش ;) and so is تَنَاوُشٌ . (Msb, and K in art. نأش .) And He took him, or reached him, to seize his beard, or his head. (ISk, S.) You say, نَاشَهُ نَوْشَةً خَفِيفَةً [ He reached him with a feeble, or slight reach, with his spear or the like]. (A.) And الرِّمَاحُ تَنُوشُهُ The spears reach him: occurring in a poem of Dureyd Ibn-Es-Simmeh. (TA.) And الظِّبَآءُ تَنُوشُ الأَرَاكَ [ The antelopes reach and take with their mouths of the trees called اراك ]; as also . (A.) A
نوش.summary_en: The root NWSH primarily means to take, reach, or obtain something, often with the hand or mouth. It can also imply clinging to something or making someone attain good or evil.

نوص.definition_en: 1 نَاصَ ذ , (M, K,) aor. يَنُوصُ , (M,) inf. n. نَوْصٌ and مَنَاصٌ (M, K) and مَنِيصٌ (M) and نَوِيصٌ (K) and نِيَاصٌ , (K, accord. to the TA,) or نِيَاصَةٌ , (accord. to a MS. copy of the K, and accord. to to the CK,) and نَوَصَانٌ , (K,) He put himself in motion: (K:) or he put himself in motion and went away. (M, TA.) You say, مَا يَنُوصُ فُلَانٌ لِحَاجَتِى Such a one does not put himself in motion for [the accomplishment of] my want. (TA.) And مَا بِهِ نَوِيصٌ There is not in him strength (S, M) and motion (or activity ], (S, TA.) -b2- نَاصَ لِلْحَرَكَةِ , inf. n. نَوْصٌ and مَنَاصٌ , He prepared himself for motion. (M, TA.) -b3- نَاصَ الفَرَسُ , (Lth, M,) inf. n. نَوْصٌ , (Lth, TA,) The horse, having his bridle pulled in, and being put in motion, raised his head; as also : (Lth, M:) o
نوص.summary_en: The root نوص (nwS) primarily means to move, stir, or prepare for motion. It also signifies turning aside, drawing back, retreating, or fleeing from something or someone.

نون.definition_en: نَوْنَةٌ ذ The dimple in the chin of a young child: (M, K:) see خُنْعُبَةٌ , and قَلْتَةٌ , and دَائِرَةٌ , and 2 in art. دسم .
نون.summary_en: The root نون (nwn) refers to the dimple in a young child's chin. It is synonymous with terms like خُنْعُبَةٌ and قَلْتَةٌ, describing this specific facial feature.

نوي.summary_en: The root نوي (nwy) primarily signifies intention, purpose, or resolve. It denotes the mental act of determining to do something, often with a clear aim or design in mind.

هاء.summary_en: The primary meaning of هاء (hA2) is to be easy, light, or contemptible. It also conveys the idea of being vile, ignominious, or despicable.

هجد.definition_en: 1 هَجَدَ ذ , (S, A, L, Msb,) aor. هَجُدَ , (L, Msb,) inf. n. هُجُودٌ ; (L, Msb, K;) and , (S, A, L, Msb, K,) and , (L, K,) and ; (IAar, L;) He (a man, L) slept: (L, Msb, K:) or slept in the night: (IAar, S, A, L:) or, in the latter part of the night. (L.) -b2- Also هَجَدَ and (S, A, L, Msb, K [in some copies, of the last of which the former verb is written and so in the TA) He remained awake, or was sleepless or wakeful in the night: (S, A, L:) and hence the praying in the night is called تَهَجُّدٌ : (S, L:) or he awoke from sleep (K) to pray, or for some other purpose: (TA:) or he prayed in the night; (Msb;) as also : (IAar, L:) thus these verbs bear two contr. significations: (S, A, L, Msb, K:) and he relinquished sleep for prayer: (A:) so in the Kur, xvii., 81. (Beyd.)
هجد.summary_en: The root هجد (hjd) primarily means to sleep, especially at night. However, it also carries the opposite meaning: to remain awake or be sleepless at night, often for prayer.

هجع.definition_en: هَجْعَةٌ ذ (assumed tropical:) The setting of a star. (Sgh in TA, art. شرط .) See شَرَطٌ . -b2- A light sleep in the first part of the night. (S.)
هجع.summary_en: The root هجع (hjE) refers to the setting of a star or a light sleep in the early part of the night. It implies a brief, initial period of rest or decline.

هدد.summary_en: The root هدد (hdd) primarily means to demolish, pull down, or destroy something, often with force or violence. It can also refer to a loud, rumbling sound, like thunder or a collapsing building.

هدم.definition_en: 1 هَدَمَ ذ He threw down, or pulled down, a building; (Msb;) pulled it to pieces; demolished it; destroyed it: (K:) [the last two explanations are the most correct, as is shown by the phrase] نَقَضَ البِنَآءَ مِنْ عَيْرِ هَدْمِ [ He took to pieces the building without demolishing, or destroying ]: (S, A, Msb, K, * in art. قوض :) he ruined [a building, &c.]; reduced [it] to ruin. (Ham, p. 31.)
هدم.summary_en: The root هدم (hdm) primarily means to demolish or pull down a building, reducing it to ruins. It implies a complete destruction, distinct from merely dismantling.

هدهد.summary_en: The primary meaning of هدهد (hudhud) refers to the hoopoe bird. It is specifically mentioned in the Quran in the context of Prophet Solomon and his communication with this bird.

هرب.definition_en: 1 هَرَبَ ذ , (S, K,) aor. هَرُبَ ; not هَرَبَ , as some have imagined on account of the measure of the first of the following inf. ns., imagining thence also that the pret. is هَرِبَ ; nor هَرَبَ with the pret. هَرَبَ , as some have supposed because of the guttural letter; for a guttural letter, when it is the first, is not reckoned as having any influence on the form of the aor.; nor هَرِبَ , as some have thought; (TA;) inf. n. هَرَبٌ (S, K) and مَهْرَبٌ and هَرَبَانٌ ; (K;) He (a man, or any animal, TA) fled; ran away. (S, K.) -b2- إِلَيْكَ مِنْكَ المَهْرَبُ [ To Thee I flee for refuge from Thee; i. e., from thy punishment: addressed to God]. (TA.) -b3- هَرَبَ مِنَ الوَتَدِ نِصْفُهُ Half of the wooden pin, peg, or stake, disappeared [ in the ground ]. (K.) -A2- هَرِبَ , aor. هَرَبَ , He
هرب.summary_en: The root هرب (haraba) primarily means "to flee" or "run away." It can refer to a person or animal escaping. A less common meaning is "to become extremely old or decrepit," similar to هرم.

هزل.definition_en: 1 هَزْلٌ ذ is contr. of جِدٌّ . (S, Mgh, K.) You say, هَزَلَ , aor. هَزِلَ , inf. n. هَزْلٌ ; (S, Msb, K;) and هَزِلَ , aor. هَزَلَ , (K,) inf. n. as above; (TA;) and ; (K;) He jested, or joked; (Msb;) or was not serious, or in earnest; (TA;) فِى كَلاَمِهِ in his speech; (Msb, TA;) and فِى الأَمْرِ in the affair. (TA.)
هزل.summary_en: The root هزل (hazala) means to jest, joke, or not be serious. It's the opposite of being earnest. It can refer to speech or an affair.

هشش.summary_en: The root هشش (hshsh) primarily means to break or crumble something dry and brittle, like dry herbage or bread.

هلع.summary_en: The root هلع (hlE) primarily describes a state of extreme impatience, anxiety, and covetousness, often accompanied by a lack of self-control.

همد.definition_en: 1 هَمَدَتِ النَّارُ ذ , aor. هَمُدَ , (S, A, L,) inf. n. هُمُودٌ , (S, A, L, K,) The fire became extinguished (As, S, A, L, K) entirely; went out entirely, (As, S, A, L,) none of it remaining: (L:) or lost its heat: (L, K:) when [only] its flame has ceased, you say of it خَمَدَت . (As, L.) -b2- هَمَدَ , (M, A, L,) aor. هَمُدَ , (M, L,) inf. n. هُمُودٌ , (M, L, K,) (tropical:) He died; [ became extinct; ] (M, A, L, K;) perished; (TA;) like as did Thamood; (Lth, A, L;) as also خَمَدَ . (A.) -b3- كَاد يَهَمْدُ مِنَ الجُوعِ (tropical:) He nearly perished of hunger. (L.) -b4- هَمَدَ , aor. هَمُدَ , (S, A, L,) inf. n. هُمُودٌ (S, L, K) and هَمْدٌ , (L, K,) (tropical:) It (a garment, or piece of cloth,) became dissundered (L, K) and worn-out, (S, A, L, Msb,) by being long folded, (A, L, Msb, K,)
همد.summary_en: The root "hmd" describes something becoming still, extinguished, or lifeless. It refers to fire dying out completely, land becoming barren, or even voices falling silent.

همر.definition_en: 1 هَمَرَهُ ذ , (S, A, K,) aor. هَمُرَ (S, K) and هَمِرَ , (K,) inf. n. هَمْرٌ , (S,) He, or it, poured it; out or forth; (S, A, K;) namely, water, (S, TA,) and tears, and rain, and the like. (TA.) -b2- هَمَرَ مَا فِى الضَّرْعِ He drew forth all the milk that was in the udder. (S, K.) -b3- هَمَرَ لَهُ مِنْ مَالِهِ (assumed tropical:) He gave to him of his property. (S, K.) -b4- هَمَرَ الكَلَامَ , (K,) or فى كَلَامِهِ , (A,) aor. هَمُرَ , inf. n. هَمْرٌ : (TA;) and بِالكَلَامِ ; (S;) (tropical:) He talked much. (K.) -A2- هَمَرَ , intrans.: see 7. in two places.
همر.summary_en: The root همر (hmr) primarily means to pour out, like water or tears. It can also refer to drawing out all the milk from an udder, giving generously from one's property, or speaking voluminously.

همس.definition_en: 1 هَمَس ذ , aor. هَمِسَ , (A, TA,) inf. n. هَمْس (AHeyth, L, TA) and هَمِيسٌ and هُمُوسٌ , (L, TA,) He spoke inaudibly: (AHeyth, TA:) or in a low, faint, gentle, or soft, manner, (AHeyth, TA,) so as to be hardly intelligible. (TA.) It is said in a trad. فَجَعَلَ بَعْضُنَا يَهْمِسُ إِلَى بَعْضٍ And some of us began to speak to others in a low, faint, gentle, or soft, manner, so as to be hardly intelligible. (TA.) And in another trad., كَانَ ذَا إِصَلَّى العَصْرَ هَمَسَ بِشَىْءٍ لَا نَفْهَمُهُ He used, when he performed the afternoon-prayer, to utter something in a low, faint, gentle, or soft manner, we not understanding it. (TA.) You say also, هَمَسَ إِلَىَّ بِحَدِيثِهِ [ He uttered his discourse to me inaudibly: or in a low, faint, gentle, or soft, manner. ] (A.) And الشَّيْطَانُ يَهْمِسُ
همس.summary_en: The root "hms" refers to speaking in a low, faint, or inaudible manner, often so soft it's hard to understand. It also describes making the slightest sound while treading, like a soft walk.

هيت.definition_en: هَيْتَ ذ an exclamation denoting wonder: the Arabs say, هَيْتَ لِلْحِلْمِ [ What forbearing mildness, or clemency! ] (L.) -b2- هَيْتَ لَكَ , (Akh, S, K, &c.,) and هَيْتِ لك , (Akh, K,) and هَيْتَ لك , (Akh, IB, K,) and the first letter is sometimes with kesreh; (K;) as is related on the authority of 'Alee, (TA,) [so that you say هِيتَ and هِيتِ and هِيتُ , the first of which three forms is mentioned by Fr, Akh, IB, and the third by Fr, IB; but for the second I find no other authority than that implied above;] of all which, the most common is هَيْتَ لك , with fet-hah to the ه and ت : (Zj:) هَيْتَ is of the dial. of Howrán, whence it became introduced into Mekkeh; and هِيتَ , of the dial. of El-Medeeneh: (Fr:) [imper. verbal ns.] i. q. هَلُمَّ , Come! (Akh, S, L, K,) or تَعَالَ the same, (Fr
هيت.summary_en: The word "hyt" is an exclamation meaning "Come!" or "Hasten!". It's used for both male and female, singular and plural.

هيل.definition_en: 7 اِنْهَالَ ذ It (sand, &c.) poured down. (S, K.) -b2- اِنْهَدَمَ الجِدَارُ وَآنْهَالَ [ The wall fell in ruins, or to pieces, or became a ruin, and broke, or crumbled down ]. (K in art. قيض .) So rendered voce اِنْقَاضَ , art. فيض .
هيل.summary_en: The root هيل (hyl) primarily describes something pouring down, like sand. It also refers to a wall collapsing or crumbling into pieces.

وأد.summary_en: The root وَأَدَ primarily means to bury alive, especially a female infant. It signifies the act of suppressing or destroying something by burying it, often with the connotation of cruelty or disgrace.

وأل.definition_en: جُوَالِقٌ جوالق and جُوَالَقٌ and جِوَالِقٌ : see art. جلق .
وأل.summary_en: The root وأل (wAl) refers to a "jawaliqun" or "jiwaliqun," which is a large sack or pannier. For a more detailed understanding, one should consult the entry for the root جلق (jlq).

وبر.definition_en: وَبْرٌ ذ , a pl. [or rather a coll. gen. n.] of which the sing. [or n. un.] is with ة ; (S, Mgh;) or a masc. n., of which the fem. is with ة , (Lth, T, M, Msb, K,) and also a pl. [or coll. gen. n.], (M,) [The hyrax Syriacus; believed to be the animal called in Hebr. שָׁפָן ;] a certain small beast, (Lth, T, S, Mgh, Msb, K,) like the cat, (Msb, K,) or of the size of the cat, (Lth, T, M, Mgh,) or smaller than the cat, (S,) of the beasts of the desert, (M,) of a dust-colour, (Lth, T, Mgh, Msb,) or of a hue between dust-colour and white, ( طَحْلَآءُ , this epithet being applied to وَبْرَةٌ , S,) or white, (TA,) having beautiful eyes, (Lth, T, Mgh,) or having eyes bordered with black, or very black eyes, ( كَحْلَآءُ , Msb,) having no tail, (S, Msb,) or having a small tail, (Mgh,) [Golius says
وبر.summary_en: The root وَبْر (wbr) refers to a small, cat-like desert animal, often called the hyrax. It's known for being shy, having no tail, and living in burrows.

وتن.definition_en: الوَتِينُ ذ [ The aorta: or the aorta descendens: ] a certain vein [ or artery ] adhering to the inner side of the backbone all along, which supplies all the [ other ] veins [ or arteries ] with blood, and irrigates the flesh, being the river of the body: or a certain thick white vein resembling a cane: [this last is the description given by Zj in his “ Khalk el-Insán: ”] or [ the aorta ascendens; ] the نِيَاط of the heart: or a certain white vein within the back of the neck: it is said to draw up [ its supply ] from the heart, and in it is the blood. -b2- Also, the خِلْب , q. v.: pl. أَوْتِنَةٌ and وُتُنٌ : (M:) i. q. نِيَاطُ القَلْبِ . (Bd, and Jel, lxix. 45.) See أَبْهَرُ .
وتن.summary_en: The root "wtn" refers to the aorta, a major artery supplying blood throughout the body. It's described as a large vein adhering to the backbone or the heart's main artery.

وجب.definition_en: 1 وَجَبَ ذ , aor. يَجِبُ , inf. n. وَجْبَةٌ (Lh, K) and وَجْبٌ (Lh) It (a wall, or the like, Msb, or a house, or anything, Lh,) fell down. (Lh, K, Msb.) See وَجْبَةٌ . -b2- وَجَبَ , inf. n. وَجْبَةٌ , It fell to the ground. (TA.) -b3- وَجْبَةٌ does not signify a single act; but is an inf. n. in an absolute sense, unrestricted to the signification of a single act: ex. وَجْبَةُ الشَّمْسِ The falling of the sun, in setting. (TA.) -b4- فَإِذَا وَجَبَتْ جُنُوبُهَا [Kur. xxii. 37,] is said to signify And when their sides fall down upon the ground: or and when their souls depart, and they fall down. (TA.) -b5- وَجَبَتِ الشَّمْسُ , (S, K,) inf. n. وَجْبٌ and وُجُوبٌ (K) and وَجْبَةٌ (see above), The sun set. (S, K.) -b6- وَجَبَتِ العَيْنُ (tropical:) The eye was, or became, sunk in the head. (K.)
وجب.summary_en: The root "wjb" primarily means to fall, as in a wall falling or the sun setting. It also describes the heart palpitating or an eye sinking.

وحش.definition_en: وَحْشٌ ذ , applied to a country, or region, (S, K,) and a place, (TA,) and a house ( داَرٌ ), (A,) and [its fem.] وَحْشَةٌ , applied to a land ( أَرْضٌ ), (S, TA,) to a house ( دار ); (A;) Desolate, deserted, or destitute of human beings or inhabitants; (S, K, TA;) as also and : (A:) and أَرْضٌ وَحْشَةٌ and signify the same. (K, TA.) You say also, بِلَادٌ حِشُونَ Countries, or regions, desolate, deserted, &c.; after the manner of سِنُونَ ; and in the accus. and gen., حِشِينَ : pl., as Az says, of , originally وَحْشٌ , [So I read instead of وَحْشَة , which is evidently a mistranscription,] the و being wanting, as it is in زِنَةٌ and صِلَةٌ and عِدَةٌ . (TA.) You also say, لَقِيتُهُ بِوَحْشِ إِصْمِتَ , (S, K,) and إِصْمِتَةَ , (TA,) i. e., I found him, or met him, in a desolate, or deserted,
وحش.summary_en: The root "wHsh" describes something desolate or wild. It refers to uninhabited lands or places, and to wild animals. It can also describe a person who is alone, solitary, or hungry.

وسن.definition_en: سَوْسَنٌ ذ , (M, Msb, K,) like جَوْهَرٌ [in measure], (Msb, K,) by the vulgar pronounced سُوسَنٌ , with damm to the first letter, (Msb, [and thus written in one of my copies of the S, in the other of those copies, and app. in most others, omitted,]) a Pers., or foreign, word, ( أَعْجَمِىٌّ ,) current in the language of the Arabs, (M,) [i. e.] an arabicized word, [app. from the Pers. سُوسَنْ , in Hebr. שׁוּשַׁן ,] (S,) [applied in the present day to The lily: and also the iris: and the pancratium: and app. to other similar flowers: ] a certain plant, (M, Msb, K, *) of sweet odour, (K,) resembling what are called رَيَاحِين , with broad leaves, but not having an odour that diffuses itself like the رياحين ; (Msb;) it is well known, and of many kinds, the sweetest of which is the white: (S: [bu
وسن.summary_en: The root "wsn" refers to the "lily" or "iris" flower, known as "سَوْسَنٌ". It's an arabicized Persian word, describing a sweet-smelling plant with broad leaves.

وشي.summary_en: The root W-SH-Y primarily means to adorn or embellish something, often with various colors or patterns.

وضن.definition_en: وَضِينٌ ذ A girth ( بِطَان ) wide, woven of thongs or hair, or only of skin; (M, K;) said to be adapted for the رَحْل and the هَوْدَج ; the بِطَان being peculiarly for the قَتَب : (M:) or it is for the هَوْدَج , like the بِطَان for the قَتَب and the تَصْدِير for the رَحْل and the حِزَام for the سَرْج ; and both [but to what this refers is doubtful] are like the نِسْع , except that they are of thongs woven one upon another, doubly or more. (S.)
وضن.summary_en: A waḍīn is a wide girth, made of woven thongs, hair, or skin. It's used for securing a camel's saddle (raḥl) or a litter (hawdaj), similar to how a biṭān is used for a pack-saddle (qatab).

وطن.definition_en: 2 وَطَّنَ نَفْسَهُ عَلَى الأَمْرِ ذ He disposed and subjected his mind, or himself, to do the thing; syn. مَهَّدَهَا لِفِعْلِهِ وَذَلَّلَهَا (Msb.) -b2- وظّن نفسه على الشَّىْءِ , and لَهُ , He induced, or persuaded, himself to do the thing; syn. حَمَلَهَا عَلَيْهِ . (ISd, in TA.) See also 5 and 10.
وطن.summary_en: The root W-T-N relates to settling or making a place home.

وفد.definition_en: 4 اوفدهُ عَلَيْهِ ذ , (M, A, K,) and إِلَيْهِ , (S, M, K,) inf. n. إِيفَادٌ ; (K;) and إِلَيْهِ , (L,) inf. n. تَوْفِيدٌ ; (K;) He sent him to him, (S, M, A, K,) namely, to a king, (A,) or governor, (S,) [or great man, as an ambassador, envoy, or messenger; or to convey gifts, and to ask aid, or assistance: see 1]. -b2- الأَمِيرُ إِلَى الأَمِيرِ الَّذِى فَوْتَهُ [ The governor, sent him as an envoy to the governor who was above him ]. (L.) -b3- مَا أَوْفَدَكَ عَلَيْنَا (assumed tropical:) [ What hath caused thee to come to us? ]. (TA.) -b4- بَيْنَمَا أَنَا فِىضِيقٍ إِذْ أَوْفَدَ ٱللّٰهُ عَلَىَّ بِرَجُلٍ فَأَخْرَجَنِى مَنْهُ (tropical:) While I was in difficulty, lo, God brought to me a man, and extricated me from it. (A.) -A2- اوفد عَلَى الشَّىْءِ , inf. n. إِيفَادٌ , (tropical:) It rose u
وفد.summary_en: The root وفد (wfd) primarily means to send someone as an envoy or messenger, often to a person of authority. It also describes something rising, overtopping, or becoming elevated.

وفر.definition_en: 1 وَفَرَ ذ , (T, S, M, A, Msb, K,) aor. يَفِرُ ; (T, Msb, K; *) and وَفُرَ , [aor. يَوْفُرُ ;] (A, K;) inf. n. وُفُورٌ (T, S, M, Msb, K) and وَفْرٌ and فِرَةٌ , (M, K,) [all of the former,] or وَفْرٌ is inf. n. of the trans. v.; (Msb;) and وَفَارَةٌ , (K,) [which is of the latter;] It (a thing) was, or became, full, complete, perfect, whole, or entire: (S, Msb:) or it (a thing, TA, or property, M, K, and herbage, M, [and a collection of goods, or commodities, or household furniture and utensils,]) was, or became, much, abundant, ample, or copious; (T, M, A, K;) not deficient: (T:) or it (anything) was, or became, generally, or universally, comprehensive: [syn., in the last sense, عَمَّ , as is implied by an explanation of the epithet وَفْرٌ , by which this sense of the verb is indicated in
وفر.summary_en: The root "wfr" means to be or make full, complete, abundant, or ample. It applies to things like property, herbage, and even one's honor or dignity.

وفض.definition_en: وَفْضَةٌ ذ A pastor's [ bag of the kind called ] خَرِيطَة , for his implements and provisions, (M, K,) which he carries therein. (M.) -b2- And hence, as being likened thereto, (M,) A [ quiver of the kind called ] جَعْبَة , (M, K,) or a thing like the جَعْبَة .
